---
title: "498 Project"  
author: "Lei Wei"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex  
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{ctex}  
  - \usepackage{graphicx}  
  - \usepackage{booktabs}  
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# NYC AIR QUALITY: FULL ANALYTICAL PIPELINE & PERFORMANCE AUDIT
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Environment & Data Ingestion
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(tidyverse)    
library(tsibble)      
library(fable)        
library(feasts)       

# Load data and ensure valid time-series structure
full_ts <- read_csv("Time_Trend.csv") %>%
  group_by(Year_Extracted, `Geo Place Name`, Name) %>%
  summarise(Value = mean(`Data Value`, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  as_tsibble(key = c(`Geo Place Name`, Name), index = Year_Extracted)

# Define Core Pollutants
core_indicators <- c("Fine particles (PM 2.5)", "Nitrogen dioxide (NO2)", "Ozone (O3)")
core_ts <- full_ts %>% filter(Name %in% core_indicators)
```


```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. PHASE A: MODEL EVALUATION (Backtesting on 2020-2023)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# We train on data up to 2019 and test against the actual 2020-2023 data
train_data <- core_ts %>% filter(Year_Extracted <= 2019)
test_data  <- core_ts %>% filter(Year_Extracted > 2019)

# Fit ARIMA and ETS models for evaluation
evaluation_fit <- train_data %>%
  model(
    arima = ARIMA(Value),
    ets = ETS(Value)
  )

# Calculate accuracy metrics across all neighborhoods and pollutants
all_performance <- evaluation_fit %>%
  forecast(h = 4) %>%
  accuracy(test_data) %>%
  select(Name, `Geo Place Name`, .model, RMSE, MAPE, MAE) %>%
  arrange(MAPE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. PHASE B: STRATEGIC FORECASTING (2024-2028)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fit models using all data up to 2023 for the most informed future projection
strategic_fit <- core_ts %>%
  model(
    arima_strat = ARIMA(Value),
    ets_strat = ETS(Value)
  )

final_forecast <- strategic_fit %>% forecast(h = 5)
```


```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. PRINT SUMMARY TABLES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TABLE 1: Top 10 Best Performing Models Overall (Lowest MAPE)
cat("\n======================================================================\n")
cat("TABLE 1: GLOBAL TOP PERFORMING MODELS (Lowest Error Rates)\n")
cat("======================================================================\n")
print(head(all_performance, 10))

# TABLE 2: Performance Audit for Core Indicators (Target Area)
target_geo <- "Downtown - Heights - Slope"
cat("\n======================================================================\n")
cat("TABLE 2: ACCURACY AUDIT FOR:", target_geo, "\n")
cat("======================================================================\n")
target_performance <- all_performance %>% 
  filter(`Geo Place Name` == target_geo) %>%
  arrange(Name, MAPE)
print(target_performance)
```


```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. COMPARATIVE VISUALIZATION (Fixed Legend)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
target_indicator <- "Fine particles (PM 2.5)"

# Pre-2020 Baseline for comparison
pre2020_baseline_fc <- evaluation_fit %>%
  filter(`Geo Place Name` == target_geo, Name == target_indicator) %>%
  forecast(h = 9)

# Plotting with wrapped legend
comparison_plot <- final_forecast %>%
  filter(`Geo Place Name` == target_geo, Name == target_indicator) %>%
  autoplot(core_ts %>% filter(`Geo Place Name` == target_geo, Name == target_indicator), 
           level = 95) +
  
  # Overlay Pre-2020 Baseline (Dashed)
  autolayer(pre2020_baseline_fc, level = NULL, linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  
  # Test Period Shading
  annotate("rect", xmin = 2020, xmax = 2023, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
           fill = "#636363", alpha = 0.1) +
  annotate("text", x = 2021.5, y = 14, label = "Test Period\n(2020-2023)", 
           color = "#525252", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", vjust = 1) +
  
  # WHO Guideline
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dotted", color = "#e41a1c", size = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 2009, y = 5.5, label = "WHO Guideline (5 µg/m³)", 
           color = "#e41a1c", size = 3, hjust = 0, fontface = "italic") +
  
  # Color Scale & Multi-Row Legend Fix
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("arima_strat" = "#2c7fb8", "ets_strat" = "#7fcdbb", 
               "arima" = "#d95f02", "ets" = "#fdb462"),
    labels = c("arima_strat" = "Strategic ARIMA (Full Data)", 
               "ets_strat" = "Strategic ETS (Full Data)",
               "arima" = "Pre-2020 ARIMA (Baseline)", 
               "ets" = "Pre-2020 ETS (Baseline)")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE, title.position = "top"),
         fill = "none") +
  
  # Labels and Theme
  labs(
    title = "Air Quality Strategic Comparison: Model Evaluation & Future Projection",
    subtitle = paste("Location:", target_geo, "| Indicator:", target_indicator),
    x = "Year", y = "Concentration (µg/m³)",
    color = "Forecasting Scenario",
    caption = "Dashed lines: Projected using only pre-2020 data | Solid lines: Projected using data through 2023."
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2009, 2028, by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 15)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 30, 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```


```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. EXECUTE PLOT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
print(comparison_plot)
```
